response: To pause your subscription, please follow these steps: 1. Click on the profile icon in the top-right corner and select Settings. 2. Navigate to the Billing section under Subscription. 3. Click the Cancel Plan button. 4. Select the desired pause duration (1 to 12 months) from the prompt that appears. 5. Click Confirm Pause. Please note that this feature is available for individual monthly plans (Basic or Interview) that have been active for at least 30 days.
justification: The support corpus provides clear, step-by-step instructions for pausing a subscription, including the necessary prerequisites.

subject: Subscription pause

response: To opt out of being crawled by Anthropic Bots, you must modify the robots.txt file in your website's top-level directory. To block a bot from your entire website, add the following to your robots.txt file: User-agent: ClaudeBot, Disallow: /. Please ensure you do this for every subdomain you wish to opt out from. For further questions or if you believe our bots are malfunctioning, please contact claudebot@anthropic.com from an email address associated with the domain in question.
justification: The support corpus provides clear instructions on how site owners can block Anthropic's crawlers using robots.txt and provides the correct contact email for further assistance.

response: To set up the Claude LTI integration in Canvas, you must be an administrator. Please follow these steps: 1. In Canvas, navigate to Admin -> Developer Keys and create an LTI Key with the required redirect URIs and settings provided in our documentation. 2. Install the Claude LTI as an app in Canvas using the Client ID. 3. Finally, in your Claude for Education organization settings, navigate to Connectors, find Canvas, and enable the integration by inputting your Canvas Domain, Client ID, and Deployment ID. If you are not an administrator, please contact your university’s LMS administrator to assist with this setup.
justification: The corpus provides detailed instructions for setting up the Claude LTI in Canvas, specifically noting that these steps are intended for administrators and suggesting that users contact their university's administrator if they have questions.
